input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-11T01:01:53.433Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to supply values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI reads the OS theme at startup and **reacts live** when the user toggles system dark mode — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
var page = this;

// SetAppThemeColor is a shorthand for Color properties
page.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#000000"),
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For non-color properties, use the generic SetAppTheme<T>
var icon = new Image();
icon.SetAppTheme<string>(Image.SourceProperty,
    "sun.png",    // light
    "moon.png");  // dark
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concept | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Live updates** | Yes — changes instantly when the OS theme switches |
| **Scale up** | Move colors into a `ResourceDictionary` with `AppThemeBinding` for reuse across pages |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme` to force a specific theme |

For larger apps, define themed colors in `App.xaml` resources and reference them by key instead of repeating hex values on every element.
